statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.

hints.text: Check the calculation of binary exponentiation.

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: The problem involves binary exponentiation. Use the property of exponentiation that $(a^m)^n = a^{mn}$.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use the property of exponentiation to calculate $a^n$ in $O(log(n))$ time. The key is to calculate $a^{n/2}$ and then use this value to calculate $a^n$ in the next step using the property.

starterCode.cpp: class Solution {public: int power(int x, int n) { if (n == 0) return 1; if (n == 1) return x; return x * power(x, n / 2); } int solve(int a, int n) { return 0; // TODO: implement } };
starterCode.java: class Solution {public int power(int x, int n) { if (n == 0) return 1; if (n == 1) return x; return x * power(x, n / 2); } int solve(int a, int n) { return 0; // TODO: implement } }
starterCode.python: class Solution: def power(self, x: int, n: int) -> int: if n == 0: return 1 if n == 1: return x return x * self.power(x, n // 2) def solve(self, a: int, n: int) -> int: return 0 # TODO: implement
starterCode.javascript: function power(x, n) { if (n == 0) return 1; if (n == 1) return x; return x * power(x, Math.floor(n / 2)); } function solve(a, n) { return 0; // TODO: implement }

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution {public: int power(int x, int n) { if (n == 0) return 1; if (n == 1) return x; return x + power(x, n / 2); } int solve(int a, int n) { return power(a, n); } };
buggyStarterCode.java: class Solution {public int power(int x, int n) { if (n == 0) return 1; if (n == 1) return x; return x + power(x, n / 2); } int solve(int a, int n) { return power(a, n); } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def power(self, x: int, n: int) -> int: if n == 0: return 1 if n == 1: return x return x + self.power(x, n // 2) def solve(self, a: int, n: int) -> int: return self.power(a, n)
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function power(x, n) { if (n == 0) return 1; if (n == 1) return x; return x + power(x, Math.floor(n / 2)); } function solve(a, n) { return power(a, n); }

functionSignature.python: def solve(self, a: int, n: int) -> int:

solutions.python: from sys import stdin class Solution: def power(self, x: int, n: int) -> int: if n == 0: return 1 if n == 1: return x while n > 1: if n % 2 == 0: n = n // 2 x = x * x else: n = n - 1 x = x * x return x def solve(self, a: int, n: int) -> int: return self.power(a, n) def main(): a, n = map(int, stdin.readline().split()) s = Solution() print(s.solve(a, n)) main()
solutions.cpp: from sys import stdin # include <iostream> # include <vector> class Solution {public: int power(int x, int n) { if (n == 0) return 1; if (n == 1) return x; while (n > 1) { if (n % 2 == 0) { n = n / 2; } else { n = n - 1; } x = x * x; } return x;} int solve(int a, int n) { return power(a, n);}};
